verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand (pro-EREG) that is anchored at the plasma membrane before ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding releases the soluble active EGF-like ligand. The TM precursor stage is real and supports juxtacrine EGFR/ERBB4 signaling, but it is short-lived and the soluble shed form is the dominant biological actor. This matches the transient-TM-precursor-of-shed-ligand pattern, qualifying as contextual via dual_localization rather than secreted_only.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface residence time of pro-EREG TM precursor is short; uncertain whether the membrane pool is therapeutically addressable vs. the dominant soluble shed form.
